title: ‘I Want Your Sex,’ ‘Girls Like Girls’ and ‘Leviticus’ Among Frameline50’s 2026 Line-Up
content:
ByPayton Turkeltaub

Gregg Araki’s “I Want Your Sex,” Hayley Kiyoko’s “Girls Like Girls” and Adrian Chiarella’s “Leviticus” are among the titles set to screen during the San Francisco International LGBTQ+ Film Festival (Frameline50), Frameline announced Tuesday. The festival will take place June 17 through 27 across venues in San Francisco, Berkeley and Oakland.

Framline50 will open with D’Arcy Drollinger’s “Lady Champagne.” Other highlights include narrative features like  John Early’s “Maddie’s Secret,” Xiaodan He’s “Montreal, My Beautiful,” Alice Maio Mackay’s “Our Effed Up World and Gary Jaffe’s “Before I Do.” Documentary screenings include David Shadrack Smith’s “Public Access,” Jeffrey Schwarz’s “Mineshaft: The Cruising Murders” and Alexandria Stapleton’s “The Brittney Griner Story.” Over 140 films from 35 countries will be screened over the course of the festival.

Colman Domingo will also be presented with theVariety Creative Conscience Awardon June 19, after which he will be joined in conversation byVariety’s Jazz Tangcay. Kai Stänicke’s “Trial of Hein” will be honored with Frameline’s annual Out in the Silence Award. underwritten by Dean Hamer and Joe Wilson, the honor shines a light on an outstanding film that highlights brave acts of LGBTQ+ visibility.

“It is an unbelievable privilege to lead this organization during our landmark 50th festival. The lead up to this anniversary has happened alongside this looming, all-pervading sense that a revolution — both political and moral — is mounting. Now, the 50th is here. And so is the revolution. Our rights are being taken away now. Attacks on our bodies and identities are happening now. And it is precisely this moment that 50 years of queer cinema matters most,” said Allegra Madsen, Frameline’s executive director. “The legacy we are celebrating now is our living inheritance and the filmmakers we are showcasing this year are expanding it, dreaming of queer futures we haven’t thought to imagine, and documenting the revolution as it happens. Frameline50 is where the two generations meet: The community that built this festival built it for this moment. This is our moment to use it.”

Tickets for all programs will be on sale on Wednesday.
title_zh: 《我想要你的性(I Want Your Sex)》、《女孩喜歡女孩(Girls Like Girls)》和《利未記(Leviticus)》將成為Frameline50在2026年的片單之一。
content_zh:
由佩頓·圖克塔布(Payton Turkeltaub)報導

格雷格·阿拉基(Gregg Araki)的《我想要你的性(I Want Your Sex)》，海莉·基奧科(Hayley Kiyoko)的《女孩喜歡女孩(Girls Like Girls)》以及阿德里安·基亞雷拉(Adrian Chiarella)的《利未記(Leviticus)》等影片將於舊金山國際LGBTQ+影展(Frameline50)期間放映，Frameline於週二宣布。該影展將於6月17日至27日在舊金山、伯克利和奧克蘭的多個場地舉行。

Frameline50將以達西·德羅林格(D’Arcy Drollinger)的《香檳女士(Lady Champagne)》開幕。其他亮點包括約翰·厄利(John Early)的敘事長片《瑪迪的秘密(Maddie’s Secret)》，何曉丹(Xiaodan He)的《蒙特利爾，我的美麗(Montreal, My Beautiful)》，艾莉斯·梅奧·麥凱(Alice Maio Mackay)的《我們的糟糕世界(Our Effed Up World)》以及加里·雅菲(Gary Jaffe)的《在我結婚之前(Before I Do)》。紀錄片放映包括大衛·沙德拉克·史密斯(David Shadrack Smith)的《公共接入(Public Access)》，傑弗瑞·施瓦茨(Jeffrey Schwarz)的《礦井：巡航謀殺案(Mineshaft: The Cruising Murders)》以及亞歷山德里亞·斯塔普頓(Alexandria Stapleton)的《布蘭妮·格萊納的故事(The Brittney Griner Story)》。在影展期間，將放映來自35個國家的超過140部影片。

科爾曼·多明戈(Colman Domingo)將於6月19日獲頒《綜藝雜誌》(Variety)創意良知獎(Variety Creative Conscience Award)，隨後將與《綜藝雜誌》的爵士·唐凱(Jazz Tangcay)進行對話。凱·斯坦克(Kai Stänicke)的《海因的審判(Trial of Hein)》將獲得Frameline每年頒發的“沉默中的出櫃獎(Out in the Silence Award)”，該獎項由迪恩·哈默(Dean Hamer)和喬·威爾遜(Joe Wilson)資助，旨在表彰一部突顯LGBTQ+可見性勇敢行為的傑出影片。

“在我們具有里程碑意義的第50屆影展期間領導這個組織是一種難以置信的特權。這個周年紀念的準備過程伴隨著一種迫在眉睫、無所不在的感覺，這場革命——無論是政治還是道德——正在醞釀中。現在，第50屆影展來了，革命也來了。我們的權利正在被剝奪，對我們的身體和身份的攻擊正在發生。正是在這一刻，50年的酷兒電影顯得尤為重要。”Frameline執行董事阿萊格拉·馬德森(Allegra Madsen)表示。“我們現在所慶祝的遺產是我們的活生生的繼承，而我們今年展示的電影製作人正在擴展這一遺產，夢想著我們尚未想像的酷兒未來，並記錄著革命的發生。Frameline50是兩代人相遇的地方：建立這個影展的社區是為了這一刻而建立的。這是我們利用它的時刻。”

所有節目的票務將於週三開售。
